proven.claim: PROVEN: when childForFieldName("property") returns null, optional chaining
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 338
; PROVEN: when childForFieldName("property") returns null, optional chaining
; prop?.text evaluates to undefined; no pattern matches; returns null.

; fn_type: 1 = "member_expression" (we entered the if-block, fn is non-null)
; prop_is_null: 1 = childForFieldName("property") returned null
(declare-const fn_type Int)
(declare-const prop_is_null Int)
(declare-const result Int)  ; 0 = null, 1 = "string", 2 = "number"

; Precondition: we passed the fn?.type === "member_expression" guard
(assert (= fn_type 1))
; Precondition: prop is null
(assert (= prop_is_null 1))

; Code transition: prop?.text → undefined; all equality checks fail
;                  fall through to: return null
(assert (=> (= prop_is_null 1) (= result 0)))

; Negation: result is not null
(assert (not (= result 0)))
(check-sat)
; expect: unsat — null prop always yields null result, contradicting result≠0

proven.claim: PROVEN: the .toString() → "string" heuristic is semantically correct and reachable.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 338
; PROVEN: the .toString() → "string" heuristic is semantically correct and reachable.
; foo.toString() is a valid call_expression; its function child is a member_expression
; with property "toString"; the code returns "string".

; fn_type:       0=null, 1="member_expression", 2=other
; prop_category: 0=null/other, 1="toString/toFixed/toUpperCase", 2="length/indexOf"
(declare-const fn_type Int)
(declare-const prop_category Int)
(declare-const result Int)  ; 0 = null, 1 = "string", 2 = "number"

; Preconditions: call_expression case, fn is member_expression, prop is "toString"
(assert (= fn_type 1))
(assert (= prop_category 1))

; Code transition: prop?.text === "toString" → return "string"
(assert (=> (and (= fn_type 1) (= prop_category 1)) (= result 1)))

; Negation: result is not "string"
(assert (not (= result 1)))
(check-sat)
; expect: unsat — .toString() call correctly yields "string", contradicting result≠1

violations.claim: VIOLATION: the comment contracts ".length → number" but for arr.length (a
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 338
; VIOLATION: the comment contracts ".length → number" but for arr.length (a
; property access), the AST value node type is "member_expression" — which
; hits default: return null. The .length → number path requires foo.length()
; (a call_expression), which is syntactically valid but semantically invalid JS
; (calling a Number as a function). The heuristic is dead code for valid inputs.

; value_node_type: 0 = "call_expression", 1 = "member_expression" (foo.length access)
(declare-const value_node_type Int)
(declare-const result Int)  ; 0 = null, 1 = "string", 2 = "number"

; Precondition: input is arr.length — a property access — which produces a
; member_expression AST node when used as a variable initializer value
(assert (= value_node_type 1))

; Code transition: switch(valueNode.type) — "member_expression" matches no
; explicit case ("call_expression", "binary_expression", literals, etc.)
; → default: return null
(assert (=> (= value_node_type 1) (= result 0)))

; Violation: code produces null (0) for .length property access
(assert (= result 0))
(check-sat)
; expect: sat — arr.length (member_expression) always returns null, not "number"
; Witness: value_node_type=1, result=0
; The comment's stated contract ".length → number" is unreachable for valid JS inputs.
; Fix: add a case "member_expression" branch that checks the property name field directly.
